stem: A IT operations data backup uses 3 parallel storage devices for redundancy. The data recoverable requires at least one storage device to function. storage device 1 has a reliability of 89%. storage device 2 has a reliability of 80%. storage device 3 has a reliability between 63% and 79% (uncertain due to limited operational data). What is the overall probability that the data recoverable?

ground_truth.answer: P(data recoverable) ranges from 0.9919 to 0.9954. Point estimate: 0.9936.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 storage devices fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.11 × 0.2 × 0.37 = 0.0081. P(works) = 0.9919.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.11 × 0.2 × 0.21 = 0.0046. P(works) = 0.9954.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
